codeReview.summary: The cross-build-verify feature successfully verified that the complete iXland monorepo build works end-to-end. All components (ixland-libc, ixland-system, ixland-wasm) build correctly in both macOS native and iOS Simulator configurations. The CMake configuration properly handles target dependencies, include paths are correctly propagated, and all static libraries are generated without linker errors.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The build verification process identified that clang-tidy is not available in the build environment, which prevents running the clang-tidy code quality checks. The build system silently handles this but it reduces the quality gate coverage.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows: 'bash: line 2: clang-tidy: command not found' when attempting to run clang-tidy on headers

sharedStateObservations.observation: The project has a pre-existing issue with .clang-format configuration that causes format.sh to fail. Workers consistently need to bypass pre-commit hooks with --no-verify flag when committing. This is noted in AGENTS.md but creates friction.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Multiple handoffs show commits with --no-verify flag due to .clang-format 'AllowShortEnumsOnASingleLine' configuration issue. The CLAUDE.md mentions this but it remains unresolved.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The CMake install target references a missing tools/test-simulator.sh file that doesn't exist in the repository, causing install to fail at the final step. This is a pre-existing issue documented in the handoff.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff discoveredIssues: 'Pre-existing: Missing tools/test-simulator.sh causes install target to fail at the end'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The monorepo build is the primary supported mode. Standalone ixland-system build has known limitations due to missing wasm-contracts headers when built outside monorepo context. This is acceptable but should be clearly documented.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff: 'Standalone ixland-system build fails due to missing wasm-contracts headers when built outside monorepo context. This is documented behavior - monorepo is the primary supported mode.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The codebase generates compiler warnings about macro redefinitions (POLLIN, POLLOUT, etc.) between linux/poll.h and system sys/poll.h. These are expected for Linux compatibility headers but could be suppressed with #pragma clang diagnostic push/pop.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff discoveredIssues notes: 'Compiler warnings about macro redefinitions... These are compatibility headers and the warnings are expected.'

summary: PASS - The cross-build-verify feature successfully validated that the complete iXland monorepo build works end-to-end. CMake configuration succeeds, all components build without errors (ixland-libc, ixland-system, ixland-wasm), 4 static libraries are created (libiox.a 96KB, libiox-runtime.a 7KB, libixland-libc-core.a 4KB, libixland-libc-usersdb.a 9KB), iOS Simulator build works, and all test executables link successfully with no undefined symbol errors. The few issues identified (missing test-simulator.sh, standalone build limitations, macro redefinition warnings) are pre-existing, non-blocking, and documented.
